questions.question: In a multi-agent virtual quant team, which agent is primarily responsible for reviewing tear sheets, examining Sharpe ratios, and flagging drawdown problems before final delivery?
options: Performance Analyst
Hypothesis Refiner
Assembler
Data Scout

questions.question: A standard risk 'kill switch' monitors whether any single outgoing order is too large. Which algorithmic behavior easily bypasses this and still causes catastrophic damage?
options: A delayed VWAP (Volume Weighted Average Price) calculation.
Submitting many tiny orders in rapid succession.
Sending one oversized block trade.
Setting an exceptionally wide stop-loss.

questions.question: Which of the following options is a clear sign of overfitting of strategy parameters?
options: Train Data Set Sharpe 1.5; Test Data Set Sharpe 1.3
Train Data Set Sharpe 0.8; Test Data Set Sharpe 0.9
Train Data Set Sharpe 0.8; Test Data Set Sharpe 0.8
Train Data Set Sharpe 1.5; Test Data Set Sharpe 0.6

questions.question: What is the false-diversification trap?
options: Assuming gold has no volatility
Assuming equal capital means equal risk
Assuming bonds always beat stocks
Assuming low correlation stays low in crises

questions.question: Which of the following is a major drawback of a Custom GPT?
options: If the instructions are too long, it may lose track of specific details.
It requires a paid subscription to create a Custom GPT.
The initial setup and configuration process is highly complex.
It is inherently difficult to formulate and create the instructions.

questions.question: Which of the following overcomes the drawback of a single Custom GPT?
options: Increasing the temperature setting to maximum.
Providing even more detailed and complex instructions.
Breaking the task into smaller multi-agent systems.
Uploading all instructions as one single large text file.
